evals.prompt: I need to create a new DHIS2 app called 'facility-registry' for managing health facilities. It should use TypeScript. I have a local DHIS2 instance running.
evals.expected_output: A fully scaffolded DHIS2 app with correct scaffold command, TanStack Query for data fetching, hash router, and popstate sync

assertions.name: uses-d2-scaffold
assertions.description: Uses pnpm create @dhis2/app@latest to scaffold (not create-react-app, @dhis2/cli-app-scripts init, or similar)

assertions.name: uses-tanstack-query
assertions.description: Installs @tanstack/react-query@4 (not v5) AND uses TanStack Query for data fetching rather than useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: hash-router
assertions.description: Uses createHashRouter from react-router-dom (not BrowserRouter, HashRouter component, or createBrowserRouter)

assertions.name: popstate-sync
assertions.description: Dispatches popstate events on route changes so the DHIS2 Global Shell URL stays in sync — any implementation that calls dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate')) in response to route/location changes

evals.prompt: Hey, I want to build a custom app for our DHIS2 instance to track vaccine supply chain data. We're running DHIS2 2.40. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step bootstrapping with correct scaffold command, TanStack Query integration, hash router, and popstate sync

assertions.name: uses-d2-scaffold
assertions.description: Uses pnpm create @dhis2/app@latest to scaffold (not create-react-app, @dhis2/cli-app-scripts init, or similar)

assertions.name: uses-tanstack-query
assertions.description: Installs @tanstack/react-query@4 (not v5) AND uses TanStack Query for data fetching rather than useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: hash-router
assertions.description: Uses createHashRouter from react-router-dom (not BrowserRouter, HashRouter component, or createBrowserRouter)

assertions.name: popstate-sync
assertions.description: Dispatches popstate events on route changes so the DHIS2 Global Shell URL stays in sync — any implementation that calls dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate')) in response to route/location changes

evals.prompt: Can you set up a new DHIS2 project for me? I need routing between a dashboard page and a details page, and I'll need to fetch data from the DHIS2 API.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded project with routing, data fetching via TanStack Query, hash router, and popstate sync
